description_long: This object is a compact solar energy infrastructure installation, featuring a rectangular array of solar panels mounted on a low-profile base. The panels are set at a slight tilt, likely to optimize sunlight capture, and are framed by a metallic structure. The base includes several raised components, possibly housing electrical equipment such as inverters or control units, and is accented with red and black elements for visibility or safety. The overall design is modular and robust, suitable for placement in urban environments such as rooftops, parking lots, or small utility areas. The structure appears to be made primarily of metal and glass, with some plastic or composite parts for insulation and protection. Its size and configuration suggest it is intended for localized power generation rather than large-scale solar farms.
description: A compact, rectangular solar panel array with a metallic frame and raised equipment housings, designed for urban solar energy generation. The structure features a slightly tilted panel surface and visible red and black safety accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the tilted solar panel array and the raised equipment housings on the base.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the narrow profile of the installation and the arrangement of the raised components.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the tilted panel surface and the modular base structure.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the equipment housings and the panel support structure.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 20 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly discernible, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with a low-profile aluminum frame and integrated equipment housings
attribute_array_configuration: single row of 3-4 rectangular panels in a linear arrangement
